_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 at 100, has risen to 141, reflecting about a 41% increase since 2015. This indicates a significant rise in this group of commodities over the period. The increase is uneven across components and has been influenced by episodic events rather than a steady climb. The composite index reflects global prices sourced from FRED and IMF data.
notes.movers: Coffee has seen the largest price increase, up 99% since 2015, followed by orange juice at 34%, wheat at 19%, and sugar at 13%. These changes are primarily due to weather events and crop diseases affecting supply in fragile soft commodity markets, such as frost and drought in arabica coffee-growing regions and citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice. These are not broad-based inflation drivers but reflect disruptions in thin, vulnerable markets.
notes.why: The headline rise is driven mainly by a few weather-sensitive crops whose supply shocks cause substantial price swings. Due to these supply constraints, prices for these commodities can remain elevated over extended periods. This dynamic underscores the importance of weather and disease factors in determining soft commodity price trends.
